factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is decelerating, primarily due to the impact of COVID-19. This decline is evident across various segments and was significantly influenced by reduced demand from commercial foodservice customers as a result of restaurant closures.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was principally driven by the impact of COVID-19... Demand from our U.S. commercial foodservice customers has slowed significantly during the quarter as restaurants were closed in many localities...
The organic revenue decline was primarily driven by the global impact of COVID-19 on our operations.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: The 2.7% organic revenue decline was broad based across our segments, compared to strong sales performance in the comparable first quarter of 2019 of 5.3% revenue growth, in which organic revenue growth was 8.3%, and the current quarter decline was also due to the slowing demand environment in several of our end-markets driven by the outbreak of COVID-19.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

factors.summary: The company is actively implementing cost reduction initiatives, including rightsizing costs, which are aimed at improving operational efficiency and reducing expenses. These actions are delivering measurable savings and are partially offsetting challenges such as reduced volumes, increased material costs, and inflation.

evidence.text: We believe that reporting rightsizing costs, which include restructuring and other charges, is important as it enables management and investors to better understand the financial impact of our broad-based cost reduction and operational improvement initiatives.

The earnings decline was driven by reduced volumes and operational inefficiencies... partially offset by the gain on sale from the disposition of AMS Chino and other broad-based cost reduction activities...
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: These benefits were partially offset by the previously discussed global impact of COVID-19 on volume, increased material costs and inflation costs.

Selling, general and administrative expenses for the three months ended March 31, 2020 decreased $21.5 million, or 5.3%, from the comparable period, primarily due to a reduction in discretionary and planned spend and benefits from rightsizing actions.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is generally positive, with improvements in segment EBITDA, gross profit, and operating margins across multiple segments. Productivity initiatives, cost reductions, and rightsizing actions are contributing to these improvements. However, there is a slight decline in earnings due to reduced volumes and operational inefficiencies in some areas, partially offset by gains from asset disposals.

evidence.text: Segment EBITDA and segment EBITDA margin are useful to investors and other users of our financial information in evaluating ongoing operating profitability as they exclude the depreciation and amortization expense related primarily to capital expenditures and acquisitions that occurred in prior years, as well as in evaluating operating performance in relation to our competitors.

Segment margin increased to 7.5% from 7.4% in the prior year quarter. The earnings decline was driven by reduced volumes and operational inefficiencies... partially offset by... gain on sale from the disposition of AMS Chino...
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: Segment operating margin increased 90 basis points to 16.9% from 16.0% as compared to the prior year quarter.

Gross profit margin increased 80 basis points to 37.0% for the three months ended March 31, 2020 from the comparable period primarily due to benefits from productivity initiatives and rightsizing costs and cost containment actions.

factors.summary: The supply chain continues to face significant disruptions due to the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to operational challenges and reduced production levels. These disruptions have impacted various aspects of the business, including manufacturing operations, distribution systems, and research and development activities.

evidence.text: The pandemic is adversely affecting, and is expected to continue to adversely affect, certain elements of our business (including our supply chain, distribution systems, production levels and research and development activities)
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has adversely impacted, and poses risks to, our business, the nature and extent of which are highly uncertain and unpredictable.

evidence.text: Actions to contain the spread of COVID-19, implemented by governments across the world, began to impact our business with the shutdown of manufacturing operations in China in February.

We also have experienced and expect to continue to experience reductions in customer demand in several of our end-markets. We expect that the social distancing measures, the reduced operational status of our suppliers and reductions in production at certain facilities will more meaningfully impact our operations in the second quarter, and general business uncertainty will continue to negatively impact demand in several of our end-markets in the second quarter, and possibly beyond.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are facing significant challenges due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which has caused global downturns, negatively impacted financial and capital markets, and disrupted supply chains and operations. The pandemic's impact is expected to continue affecting segment results and economic growth, with uncertainties in the broader economic environment.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic has adversely impacted, and poses risks to, our business, the nature and extent of which are highly uncertain and unpredictable.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic has adversely impacted, and poses risks to, our business, the nature and extent of which are highly uncertain and unpredictable.

evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: The global COVID-19 pandemic is expected to significantly impact segment results in the second quarter.
The recent outbreak of COVID-19 has resulted and will continue to result in significant economic disruption and has and will adversely affect our business, including our supply chain and operations.

factors.summary: Near-term risks are significant and primarily stem from the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, which continues to disrupt operations, reduce demand, and strain financial conditions. The risks are characterized by high uncertainty regarding the duration and severity of these disruptions, with potential extended impacts on economic activity, supply chains, and business operations.

evidence.text: the pandemic could lead to an extended disruption of economic activity and the impact on our consolidated results of operations, financial position and cash flows could be material.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on the company is mixed. While the COVID-19 pandemic has increased the cost of capital and reduced access to capital markets, affecting the company negatively, other areas indicate that interest rates are not a significant factor in the company's performance and that the impact is manageable.

evidence.text: the continued spread of COVID-19 has led to disruption and volatility in the global capital markets, which increases the cost of capital and adversely impacts access to capital including the commercial paper markets.

evidence.text: The effective tax rates for the three months ended March 31, 2020 and 2019 were 17.4% and 23.6%, respectively. The decrease in the effective tax rate for the three months ended March 31, 2020 relative to the prior comparable period was primarily driven by favorable audit settlements.

factors.summary: Backlog orders are increasing across most segments, with notable growth in heat exchanger and aluminum can shaping equipment businesses. However, Fueling Solutions experienced a smaller increase compared to other segments.

evidence.text: Segment backlog increased 14.3% over the prior year quarter, driven mainly by increases in our heat exchanger and aluminum can shaping equipment businesses.
Segment backlog increased 0.6% compared to the prior year quarter.

evidence.text: Backlog as of March 31, 2020 was $1.6 billion, an increase from $1.4 billion from the prior year.

factors.summary: The company is reducing capital expenditures across multiple areas, including investing activities, fueling solutions, and overall cost containment measures, in response to the economic impacts of COVID-19. While strategic initiatives are not being deferred, investments are being re-prioritized to manage costs cautiously.

evidence.text: We have significantly reduced our capital spending plan for the year as a result of COVID-19, without deferring strategic ongoing initiatives...
The cost reduction actions include ... re-prioritization of all planned investments and hiring plans.

We also continue to monitor government economic stabilization efforts and expect to participate in certain legislative provisions, such as deferring estimated tax payments to later in the year or the following two years and utilizing job retention subsidies.

evidence.text: We have suspended repurchases as a result of business uncertainty related to COVID-19.
The cost reduction actions include ... re-prioritization of all planned investments and hiring plans.

factors.summary: The company is engaged in active M&A activities, with both acquisitions and divestitures occurring. Despite efforts to reduce capital spending due to COVID-19, strategic initiatives and acquisitions continue to be pursued, indicating a mixed approach where cost-cutting measures are balanced with strategic growth moves.

evidence.text: We have initiated a plan to significantly reduce our capital spend for the year as a result of COVID-19, without deferring strategic ongoing initiatives...
Acquisition-related growth was driven by the acquisition of SoftPak.
During the three months ended March 31, 2020, we acquired Sys-Tech Solutions, Inc. and So. Cal. Soft-Pak, Incorporated. We also sold AMS Chino, generating a gain on sale.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a favorable impact from pricing, with some variation in the extent of the impact across different subsections. While one subsection highlights a 1.6% favorable impact, another indicates a 0.7% favorable impact.

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 1.6% in the first quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 0.7% in the first quarter of 2020.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting, with higher-margin segments contributing positively, though there are regional declines in Europe and Asia due to operational and demand challenges from the pandemic.

evidence.text: Segment EBITDA margin increased to 20.0% from 14.8%

The decline in Europe was broad-based across four segments, except Pumps and Process Solutions, as the region faced significant operational and demand headwinds from the developing pandemic. The decline in Asia was driven mainly by a sharp decline in China and weakness in India across all segments as a result of the outbreak of COVID-19.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with growth observed in biopharma and hygienic sectors, particularly influenced by the COVID-19 pandemic, while other markets like commercial refrigeration, vehicle service, and apparel and textile printing are experiencing weakening demand due to a weak economic environment.

evidence.text: Growth in the biopharma and hygienic markets (including for products directly used in the fight against the COVID-19 pandemic).

We expect a weak demand environment in the second quarter and likely into the third quarter in several of our end-markets, such as the commercial refrigeration, vehicle service and apparel and textile printing markets.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to the impact of COVID-19, with the company partially offsetting these increases through productivity actions and cost containment measures.

evidence.text: These benefits were partially offset by the previously discussed global impact of COVID-19 on volume, increased material costs and inflation costs.

Gross profit for the three months ended March 31, 2020 decreased $11.3 million, or 1.8%, from the comparable period, primarily due to an organic revenue decline of 2.7%, increased material and inflation costs due to the impact of COVID-19, partially offset by pricing initiatives, benefits from productivity initiatives and rightsizing actions and cost containment actions.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with the implementation of cost containment initiatives and proactive cost reduction actions. The company is focusing on reducing non-essential expenses, re-prioritizing investments and hiring plans, and adjusting headcount to align with demand.

evidence.text: The cost reduction actions include the elimination of non-essential travel, third party and other expenses, and re-prioritization of all planned investments and hiring plans.

We are taking proactive cost reduction efforts as we monitor the impact of COVID-19. We expect the second quarter to require continued and more significant actions to reduce costs to offset revenue decline and under-absorption of production fixed overhead, such as further reducing discretionary spend and headcount to match demand.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving as a result of restructuring, efficiency initiatives, footprint consolidation, operational optimization, rightsizing actions, and cost reduction measures. The improvements are evident in both operational efficiency and financial metrics such as gross profit margin.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily driven by productivity actions including the benefit of rightsizing actions and cost reduction initiatives.

Gross profit margin increased 80 basis points to 37.0% for the three months ended March 31, 2020 from the comparable period primarily due to benefits from productivity initiatives and rightsizing costs and cost containment actions.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing due to lower demand and operational disruptions, with some facilities temporarily closed or operating at reduced capacity.

evidence.text: The reduction led to lower manufacturing utilization at our marking and coding operations in France.

As of April 17, 2020, 7% of our major global facilities (by count) were closed completely and 11% were partially closed or operated with a reduced capacity. Meanwhile, certain facilities that were closed as of March 31, 2020 have re-opened.

factors.summary: Forward guidance is cautious, reflecting ongoing challenges due to COVID-19 impacts and operational curtailments. Management anticipates continued negative impacts on segment results and end-market demand in the second quarter, with potential effects extending beyond.

evidence.text: We expect the operational curtailments and demand deferrals to continue into the second quarter and to have a negative impact on segment results.

We expect that the social distancing measures, the reduced operational status of our suppliers and reductions in production at certain facilities will more meaningfully impact our operations in the second quarter, and general business uncertainty will continue to negatively impact demand in several of our end-markets in the second quarter, and possibly beyond.

factors.summary: Management is exhibiting caution due to the ongoing challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic, which is expected to significantly impact segment results. The company is taking measures to reduce controllable costs, including furloughs and cutting back on non-essential travel and discretionary spending.

evidence.text: The global COVID-19 pandemic is expected to significantly impact segment results in the second quarter.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: We are taking steps across the portfolio and at the corporate center to reduce our controllable costs, including short-term actions to reduce labor costs, including furloughs, eliminating non-essential travel and reducing discretionary spend.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange, economic conditions, and supply chain disruptions are key sensitivities. The company experienced an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation, affecting its organic growth and bookings.

evidence.text: unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2020 Compared to the First Quarter 2019

evidence.text: Included in this result was organic growth of 0.9%, acquisition-related bookings growth of 1.0%, offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 1.2% and a 0.7% impact due to dispositions. Organic bookings growth was primarily due to robust activity in our Fueling Solutions segment as well as growth in our Pumps & Process Solutions segment, offset by a decline in our Refrigeration & Food Equipment and Engineered Products segments.

factors.summary: The company has multiple potential catalysts, including new product launches and the integration of acquisitions, which were highlighted by the acquisition of SoftPak. Additionally, cost savings from restructuring and productivity initiatives are expected to contribute positively, though these benefits are partially offset by challenges such as reduced volumes due to the COVID-19 impact and increased material and inflation costs.

evidence.text: Acquisition-related growth was driven by the acquisition of SoftPak.
These benefits were partially offset by reduced volume due to the impact of COVID-19 and increased material and inflation costs.

factors.summary: The dealer channel appears to be in a healthy state, as supported by strong orders for consumables and no specific issues mentioned in inventory levels.

evidence.text: Strong orders for consumables in our marking and coding business more than offset reductions in equipment and services bookings in marking and coding and reduced bookings in our digital printing business.
No specific information on dealer/distributor inventory levels was provided in the text.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are decelerating in several end markets, driven by reduced demand and the impact of the COVID-19 outbreak. The decline is particularly noticeable in the oil & gas sector due to falling oil prices, and broader market weakness is affecting organic revenue growth.

evidence.text: The fall in the price of oil has led to a reduction in demand from customers in the oil & gas end-market for rotating and reciprocating machinery components and aftermarket services.

The 2.7% organic revenue decline was broad based across our segments, compared to strong sales performance in the comparable first quarter of 2019 of 5.3% revenue growth, in which organic revenue growth was 8.3%, and the current quarter decline was also due to the slowing demand environment in several of our end-markets driven by the outbreak of COVID-19.

factors.summary: Construction and infrastructure spending is driving demand across various sectors, particularly in the Pumps & Process Solutions segment which serves capital-intensive industries. There is a positive outlook due to growing construction activity and demand for products used in facility cleaning, sanitizing, and chemical processing. The evidence highlights the constructive activity in the plastics and polymer processing business, which supports long-term capital projects globally. Additionally, the segment includes businesses in biopharma, medical, and chemical markets, indicating a diversified demand driven by infrastructure spending. Overall, the sentiment is positive as construction spending is fostering growth in multiple industrial sectors.

evidence.text: Activity in our plastics and polymer processing business remained constructive as it primarily serves large long-term capital projects around the world.

Our Pumps & Process Solutions segment includes businesses in the biopharma and medical markets that produce components that are used in the research and production of pharmaceuticals. Other businesses provide products for diluting and dispensing chemicals specifically used for facility cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and components that support the safe operation of paper production plants, chemical facilities, fuel refineries and many other industries and equipment that supports the recycling of plastic waste.
